summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened by addressing the spiritual warfare believers face, calling the church to stand strong and unified rather than conforming to culture. He then preached a message titled 'I'm so fragile, but he is so faithful,' acknowledging the common human experience of fragile faith while celebrating God's unwavering faithfulness. The sermon centered on three key reminders for those feeling spiritually distant. First, no one is ever too far gone for God's grace, which outruns our mistakes. Pastor Travis illustrated this with biblical examples of wanderers like the Israelites, David, and Jonah, whom God faithfully pursued and restored. Second, we are always one choice away from changing our life's direction. He emphasized that true repentance involves both telling God we're sorry and turning away from sin, using the parable of the prodigal son as a powerful example of coming to our senses and returning to the Father. Third, our personal stories are ultimately for God's glory. He leads and restores us 'for his name's sake,' marking us as His own. The message concluded with a call to repentance and a prayer for those who have wandered, whether needing to return to God or give their lives to Jesus for the first time, highlighting that a single choice to turn around can lead us home.
mainPoints: No matter how far you have wandered, you are never too far gone for God's faithful grace and pursuit.
You are one choice—a decision to truly repent and turn around—away from completely changing your life's direction.
Your story of wandering and restoration is not just about you; it is for God's glory and proves His faithfulness.

versesMentioned.text: When he came to his senses, he said, ‘How many of my father’s hired servants have food to spare, and here I am starving to death!’
he refreshes my soul. He guides me along the right paths for his name’s sake.
Greater love has no one than this: to lay down one’s life for one’s friends.
If you declare with your mouth, “Jesus is Lord,” and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.

Monday.scripture: Psalm 23:3 (NIV) - he refreshes my soul. He guides me along the right paths for his name’s sake.
Monday.action: Identify one area where you feel spiritually distant or 'in the wilderness.' Write it down.
Monday.prayer: God, thank you that you guide me. Help me to see where I have wandered and trust that you are leading me back for your name's sake.

Tuesday.scripture: Luke 15:17 (NIV) - When he came to his senses...
Tuesday.action: Reflect on the 'pig slop' or difficult circumstance in your life. Ask God how He might be using it to bring you back to Him.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, open my eyes today. Help me to 'come to my senses' about any area where I am wasting the resources or blessings you have given me.

Wednesday.scripture: Isaiah 64:6 (NIV) - All of us have become like one who is unclean, and all our righteous acts are like filthy rags...
Wednesday.action: Perform a simple act of surrender. Tell God, 'I cannot do this on my own. I need you to be the Lord of my life today.'
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, I confess my own efforts are not enough. I surrender control and ask you to clothe me in your righteousness today.

Thursday.scripture: Luke 15:20 (NIV) - But while he was still a long way off, his father saw him and was filled with compassion for him; he ran to his son...
Thursday.action: If you need to 'turn around,' take one practical step back toward God (e.g., open your Bible, pray honestly, reach out to a believer).
Thursday.prayer: Thank you, Father, that you run to me when I turn back. I choose to turn around today. I receive your compassion and forgiveness.

Friday.scripture: Romans 10:9 (NIV) - If you declare with your mouth, “Jesus is Lord,” and believe in your heart... you will be saved.
Friday.action: Declare your faith out loud. Say, 'Jesus, you are my Lord.' If you have never done this, take this step of salvation today.
Friday.prayer: Jesus, I believe in my heart you are Lord. I confess with my mouth that you are my Savior. Thank you for saving me and making me new.

Saturday.scripture: John 15:13 (NIV) - Greater love has no one than this: to lay down one’s life for one’s friends.
Saturday.action: Do one thing today that serves someone else in your church or community, reflecting God's love rather than a self-centered focus.
Saturday.prayer: God, my story is for your glory. Help me to lay down my self-interest today and love others as you have loved me.
